I don’t know if I would buy an other one - it is a lot of money and right now it it requires quite a bit of trust that it will be better. I mean, we all hope that it will get better and all the promises are that it will be better. But so far some essential things are missing and then I’m referring to communication. The poor communication brings so much uncertainty, that for me I am hoping that will get better but looking at the way things are communicated. I am a little hesitant.  
Having said all of those things, of course I’m keeping my fingers crossed and I’m hoping that the project will actually deliver what it says it will, so I fully understand the wanting to buy another one IF the promises come true. So I guess it all comes down to how much trust do you have and that it will deliver.

Unity brings together:
• Global device diversity
• Wi-Fi + cellular intelligence

A live, functioning global network expanding across tens of thousands of devices, cities, and countries.

Josh said today in the Minutes AMA, the team started pushed forward with the hyper scaling method because they realized some of the other larger jobs with governments and such were going to take longer to mature. author: Mat
msg:
@defiants_kay one for the team please:

To keep the market stable, it feels important that a UNO should only be able to revoke a lease if the ULO fails to meet the stated requirements (uptime or any other terms agreed at the start of a lease).

**Question**: Is there a specific reason UNO operators can currently revoke a lease without providing a reason?

**Request**: Please add restrictions on revoking as soon as possible to support fair and predictable behaviour across the market.

author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - Round 1 Extension & Privileges**

Following direct feedback from members of the community, we have decided to extend participation in Round 1 by a further 30 days.

This extension provides additional time to access the Round 1 Privileges available in this round. Participation remains open.

**Round 1 Privileges scale with every Unity Node you own:**

**Network Credit Activation Discounts** - starting at 5% and increasing with every node, up to 32% off the $1.99/month per license.

1 Node → 5% → $1.89/License
2 Nodes → 8% → $1.83/License
3 Nodes → 11% → $1.77/License
4 Nodes ⭐️ → 14% → $1.71/License
5 Nodes → 17% → $1.65/License
6 Nodes → 20% → $1.59/License
7 Nodes ⭐️ → 23% → $1.53/License
8 Nodes → 26% → $1.47/License
9 Nodes → 29% → $1.41/License
10 Nodes ⭐️ → 32% → $1.35/License

**Bonus Unity Operator Licenses** - receive additional licenses at every tier, up to 300 bonus licenses, capped at 10 Unity Nodes. More licenses equal more network incentives.

1 Node → +5 Licenses → 205 total
2 Nodes → +25 Licenses → 425 total
3 Nodes → +50 Licenses → 650 total
4 Nodes ⭐️ → +100 Licenses → 900 total
5 Nodes → +125 Licenses → 1,125 total
6 Nodes → +150 Licenses → 1,350 total
7 Nodes ⭐️ → +200 Licenses → 1,600 total
8 Nodes → +225 Licenses → 1,825 total
9 Nodes → +250 Licenses → 2,050 total
10 Nodes ⭐️ → +300 Licenses → 2,300 total

⭐️ = Bonus Tier (larger license jump)

Every Unity Node you add in Round 1 increases your discount AND your capacity. Every Unity Node purchased in Round 1 unlocks these privileges.

These Round 1 Privileges are solely available during Round 1.

We have not yet revealed all Round 1 Privileges. What remains is arguably the most significant. Round 1 Operators with 2 Unity Nodes or more should pay close attention. More details will follow.

🔗 https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2018817955019006261

author: Byron
msg:
I would really like to know, from what part/members of the community they took direct feedback from? ... If I am not mistaken, the vast majority of the community is in here. In this very TG. And in here, there is radio silence from the team for a very long time.

Of course I do not expect an answer to my question. Since we do not get honest and direct answers from the team for quite some time now. Communication is a total disappointment at this point.

Nevertheless, this move was expected. As I have stated a couple of days ago, Unity Nodes do not sell, and they will continue to not sell with this level of performance. It was totally unjustified, and naive, to double the price. I am happy that they extended phase1, but they should have extended it indefinitely, up until revenue justified the price tag. Extending it by just 30 days will just make things explosive again since, I do not believe that in 30 days much progress will be made. Most probably, the exact opposite.

author: Danny | World Market | ENO UK
msg:
I don’t see how it’s fair to change the terms of a rewards structure after people have already made purchases based on the original criteria?

Some community members bought additional Unity Nodes specifically because the team stated that 3 nodes were required to qualify for extra Round 1 rewards. Changing that requirement after the fact fundamentally alters the basis on which those purchases were made.

When terms are changed retroactively, affected buyers are reasonably entitled to expect either differentiation, compensation, or refunds for purchases that were made under conditions that no longer apply.

This isn’t about slowing growth or blocking accessibility — it’s about basic fairness and protecting early supporters who acted in good faith on the information provided at the time.

If trust is to be maintained long-term, there needs to be a clear acknowledgement of this and a remedy for those impacted, should they want one

author: Depins
msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - Round 1 Extension & Privileges**

Following direct feedback from members of the community, we have decided to extend participation in Round 1 by a further 30 days.

This extension provides additional time to access the Round 1 Privileges available in this round. Participation remains open.

**Round 1 Privileges scale with every Unity Node you own:**

**Network Credit Activation Discounts** - starting at 5% and increasing with every node, up to 32% off the $1.99/month per license.

1 Node → 5% → $1.89/License
2 Nodes → 8% → $1.83/License
3 Nodes → 11% → $1.77/License
4 Nodes ⭐️ → 14% → $1.71/License
5 Nodes → 17% → $1.65/License
6 Nodes → 20% → $1.59/License
7 Nodes ⭐️ → 23% → $1.53/License
8 Nodes → 26% → $1.47/License
9 Nodes → 29% → $1.41/License
10 Nodes ⭐️ → 32% → $1.35/License

**Bonus Unity Operator Licenses** - receive additional licenses at every tier, up to 300 bonus licenses, capped at 10 Unity Nodes. More licenses equal more network incentives.

1 Node → +5 Licenses → 205 total
2 Nodes → +25 Licenses → 425 total
3 Nodes → +50 Licenses → 650 total
4 Nodes ⭐️ → +100 Licenses → 900 total
5 Nodes → +125 Licenses → 1,125 total
6 Nodes → +150 Licenses → 1,350 total
7 Nodes ⭐️ → +200 Licenses → 1,600 total
8 Nodes → +225 Licenses → 1,825 total
9 Nodes → +250 Licenses → 2,050 total
10 Nodes ⭐️ → +300 Licenses → 2,300 total

⭐️ = Bonus Tier (larger license jump)

Every Unity Node you add in Round 1 increases your discount AND your capacity. Every Unity Node purchased in Round 1 unlocks these privileges.

These Round 1 Privileges are solely available during Round 1.

We have not yet revealed all Round 1 Privileges. What remains is arguably the most significant. Round 1 Operators with 2 Unity Nodes or more should pay close attention. More details will follow.

author: Mat
msg:
Nothing beyond setting power management to not stop the app and also locking it so it's not closed in a close all action. 
main point is it only works well when plugged in all the time.

Specifics:
1. Disable Battery Optimization for Apps 
This prevents Android from automatically shutting down apps in the background. 
Go to Settings > Battery.
Tap on More settings or Battery usage.
Select the app you want to keep running.
Choose Don't optimize or set to Unrestricted. 

2. Lock Apps in the Recent Apps Screen
Locking an app ensures it stays in memory and isn't closed when you press "Close All". 
Open the app you want to keep running.
Open the Recent Apps screen (swipe up and hold, or press the button with three vertical lines).
Tap the two dots/menu icon in the top right corner of the app card.
Select Lock. A lock icon will appear on the app. 

3. Disable Automatic App Launch (If apps are crashing/restarting) 
Oppo's ColorOS may be preventing apps from auto-starting. 
Go to Settings > Apps > Auto-launch.
Toggle OFF the restriction for apps you want to keep running.

author: Jon
msg: I don’t think it’s manufactured outrage. I fomo’d in late October to buy a second node using a credit card because supposedly round 1 was going to end. I even told people to get in before round 2 started.  Those people feel sorry for me now and think I’m a dumb ass and I don’t blame them. I thought I would’ve been able to pay it off in a short while because of how we were misled to believe that we would be making really good $ and would hit the ground running because Unity had all their ducks in a row with partnerships and all. Y’all never hinted once that we’d be making pennies a day but quite the opposite. Why? Because you wanted to sell those nodes just like now. The app was supposedly ready and good to go but some “cataclysmic event” happened which ended up giving us a barebone not even close to being approved from the AppStore or google playstore. Y’all misled us to believe that the app was ready for the playstore/appstore at the beginning. False promises with supposed tasks (plural) rolling out in January with not a single one. This Omri guy (whatever his name is) saying how he hears us and how much he’s going to be communicating with us from now on supposedly on a weekly basis. Hell, Josh was more on point than this guy. All this dude talked about was S&R which has been a big nothing burger so far along with the caller ID task. Whatever happened to the AMA? Supposed to have been weeks ago. It’s not just “this one”but the accumulation of constant delays and gaslighting. I’ve been trying to be patient but it never ends. The only thing you guys did right was increase the $0.01-$0.04 rewards to $0.04-$0.12. I feel like the sentiment was, “We’ll forgive them for not rolling out tasks in January like they said because for sure they’ll be here by Feb 4, just a little bit longer. Because why the hell would somebody buy a node for 10k with these crumbs we’re getting, so obviously tasks are coming.” Who cares about bonus licenses if I can’t lease any of them out? Perhaps to you it’s just “this one” but for me it’s just adding on to the pile of 💩 that we’ve been dealing with and I’m certain I left out much more.

author: Abdus
msg:
Hello, I’ve got to rectify something mentioned yesterday. Maybe no one cares at this point but here goes. Had a follow up conversation with someone from Pakistan about salaries etc and I got my numbers wrong yesterday. Seems like a labourer’s salary is on average around 40k rupees which translates to approximately 140 dollars. Clerical jobs around 60-80k rupees. Just saying my numbers were off yesterday. 

Anyways tried to explain Unity and the minute crypto is mentioned, I get the scam response. So try to explain WM and MNT operations in Pakistan and they have clearances from regulatory authorities to operate. That didn’t really calm any of their qualms. 

Even though I kept explaining that we’re not asking for money. We are rewarding for tasks. Conversation went round in circles, oh but alot of these crypto companies just fail or steal the money. 

Again explained not taking from you, we are rewarding for performing certain tasks to verify the network. Again but these companies fail. So I explained again that’s the nature of startups. Some fail. Again but I will lose money. Again explain. Then used Uber as an example. I asked even what about Uber. Response: Uber big company. My response: was a start-up and made itself a name. 

Final response I got is I am the consumer. Why should I care about telephony/mobile/network verifications.   Explained again that the app can be installed and could run with minimum interactions if the user wishes to do so. 

This is a special case. Someone I know who just likes to argue about everything. Same guy was peddling whatsapp groups to earn crypto last year and I told him those were scams.

Just an insight on the kind of logic, perception and paradigm shift in mentality that is needed. Even in countries where WM and MNT are operational.

I am sure I am missing out on a couple of other things but you get the gist of this post. Bottom line there will be some tough nuts to crack. Pun intended.

N.B: This was not autogenerated by AI.

author: Mat
msg:
TLDR
The poster corrects their earlier Pakistan salary figures: labourers earn about 40k PKR (~US$140) and clerical jobs 60–80k PKR.

They then describe a circular argument with someone in Pakistan who dismissed Unity/WM/MNT as “crypto scams” the moment crypto was mentioned. Even after explaining that tasks don’t require payment, have regulatory approval, and simply reward users for network verification, the person kept repeating that “crypto companies fail” and “I will lose money.”

Analogies didn’t help, and the person insisted they had no reason to care about network verification. Ironically, they had pushed actual scam WhatsApp crypto groups last year.

The takeaway: some people will be extremely hard to convince — real “tough nuts to crack.”

N.B: This was auto summerised by AI.

author: Sophie | I will never DM you first
msg:
The Node contract allows you to enumerate all nodes - In other words, you can get a list of all node IDs.
You can then look up the number of licenses attached to a node using its ID.

Finally, you can get a total count of detached licenses from the License contract.

A combination of those three will give a total license count.

author: Byron
msg:
Usually when we say "early" when referring to a time period in a month, we mean in the first 5 - 10 days TOPS. After the 10th and towards the 15th we usually say "mid" month. After the 20th and towards the 25th we are in "late" and after the 25th and toward the 30th we say "end of month".

But all this is for normal people, speaking plain English. Unfortunately in here, mods, ambassadors and team, are not. So by "early" Sophie could mean ... well I do not know what she could mean! Take your guess. Also she doesn't mention a year. Keep that in mind.

author: VeganCatLover
msg:
One thing I did the other day was log into my router. It was configured to use both the 2.4ghz and 5ghz frequencies.
If you rename the SSIDs for each frequency then they will both appear as "Available networks" on wifi (instead of one network for both).
I then made sure my Unity devices were using the 2.4ghz network (as some are too old to use 5ghz and I don't think Unity tasks need a quick connection).
I haven't had a crash since.
Maybe your ULOs are having a similar issue (although getting them each to check and configure this might be a stretch even if it is the issue!).

Thanks for your message and for your patience.
We’ve identified and resolved an issue that may have caused your previous connection attempt to be incorrectly blocked. Everything should be working normally now.
Please try the following:
 • Close the app completely
 • Reopen it and try again
 • If you are using a VPN or proxy, please disconnect it and retry, as these connections are not supported
If you still have any trouble after this, just let us know and we’ll be happy to help further.
Best regards,

author: Pilla
msg:
Them falling is also not in our control currently. There is a some division between the team and community, but that is also not in our control

Communication is happening, mostly one way. Apart from a few questions answered, most of the comms from the team comes through announcements and AMAs

We are asking for a more informal communication from the team, it always “listened too”, but the comms remain the same. 

The negative feelings grow from the unknowns. Going to and dealing with ULOs with a lot of unknowns.

Like the CLI task being active for weeks(?) but no calls.

When we are asked about it, the only answer we have to offer is “I don’t know”

S&R updates? I don’t know

When increase in rewards? I don’t know

Why decrease in rewards? I don’t know.

A lot of the negatively would not exist, if people were more informed. It’s hard to manage ULOs currently.

author: J. P. Costa
msg:
I strongly agree with the sentiment of some here that we should have closed UNO calls with the devs/heads of team in a timely manner, just like the EarthNodes owners have, because with respect, these twitter Amas dont cut it for us.
The reason this group can get so much agitated during issues such as missing deadlines, lower rewards, lack of tasks is because we are always let in the dark. Questions such as: What happened to CLI calls that no one got a single one yet? What is necessary to increase daily telemetry rewards? SnR, wifi tasks, Ethereum mint, where are the weekly catch ups from Omri and so on?
We dont have any explanation for these up to date, missing deadlines is fine and it happens, but as long as we know what is going on and what is being worked, maybe ppl would react in total different way. author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg:
1. When they released the CLI task, why did they not tell us the expected time scale before calls would start? On that matter, why did they release it so far before jobs came in?
2. When they released the app on Android, why did they not tell us the expected time scale before proof of work would start on Android? 
3. When they released the IOS app, why did they not tell us the expected time scale, or conditions which needed to be met, before proof of work would be activated?
4. What was the outcome of the vote on the split and when will it be implemented assuming it is going to be implemented. 
5. Why haven't they told us the time scale they expect for the start of Scout and Runner alpha testing or told us the conditions that are necessary for that to start?
6. Why do they not tell us when they encounter difficulties, hurdles or other significant problems? I would enjoy following them on the journey and cheering them on. They only seem to tell us when there's really no choice in the matter. For instance, delays in Nigeria, lack of adoption of Jingle by apps. The answer may be something like they don't want to spook the market and that's okay if that's the answer, knowing that would help to manage our expectations of being told anything about strategic issues that they are facing. 
7. What percentage of the rewards we are receiving are coming from outside partners and contracts other than from world mobile?
8. What are the conditions necessary for clients to start paying for the services delivered by the edge Network, by task?
9. What value does the current proof of work task actually provide and to who? Is that information/data currently being sold to other parties other than World mobile and minutes Network? 
10. We were told that Omri was going to be communicating with us weekly. Why hasn't that happened? 

There are 10 questions I have off the top of my head. I would genuinely love to to hear the team answer these in full or at least tell us that these are not the sorts of questions that they are comfortable answering. 

Perhaps others have other questions to add?

author: OSUN
msg:
Sorry. I know I’m new here but after skimming through the past few days, I had to put my two cents in. 
Have any of you ever tried developing a project like this with hundreds of “employees” (because that’s technically what we are now) bitching and sending emails and asking stupid questions?  I haven’t, but I’ll bet you it’s a nightmare. And I’ll be the first to admit that I’m the one emailing stupid questions. 
I absolutely agree that they should be communicating more with us, but I also understand that if there’s nothing to say then there’s nothing to say. 
We need to keep a few things in mind. 
1) This is an investment. Don’t get ahead of ourselves. Take your time. Run the numbers on small samples. See what’s producing the most rewards and what’s not. But be ready for when it’s fully functional 
2) It’s in development. 
I’m a contractor. Things never meet scheduled deadlines and options are going to change. 
3) We literally are employees of Unity. We need to start acting accordingly, and so does unity.

author: James_WM_ENO
msg:
Unity Network is now ready to accommodate international and domestic Caller ID verification testing.

What is CLI Testing?
Caller ID (CLI) verification confirms that inbound calls display the correct and expected caller information as they pass through carrier routes into your device.

This is real-world telecommunications verification - validating route integrity across retail and wholesale voice paths globally.

How it works:
• Opt-in to the CLI task from the Task Tab in the Unity App
• Receive an inbound test call
• You can ignore the call or answer - if answered, the call will disconnect immediately
• Missed the call? That's ok - submit the number you saw on the inbound call at any time within 24 hours

Network Incentives:
From 0.05 UP to 2.00 UP per call, depending on test parameters.

What's ahead:
CLI verification volume on the network is expected to increase as commercial demand scales. SMS CLI verification is coming soon, amongst additional native & opt-in tasks. Opt-in now via the Unity App to start verifying CLI

author: Mat
msg:
These kind of parameters?
• RSRP — enough signal strength so the phone can register and signal the call.
• RSRQ / SINR — signal quality must be good enough for control-plane signaling.
• Latency — signaling messages must travel quickly enough to complete call setup.
• Handovers (during setup) — only relevant if a cell change occurs while dialing.
• IMS registration state (for VoLTE/VoNR) — the device must be registered for IP voice.
• Call setup success rate (core KPI) — reflects MSC/IMS responsiveness.
• Signaling bearer establishment (RRC + SIP/ISUP) — control channels must form correctly.

author: Quoia75
msg:
**Is anyone up for a discussion on how to manage comms with ULOs today?**


Just read in the old TG that there will be **no dedicated Discord or Telegram channel for ULOs**. That means UNOs will be primarily responsible for providing ULO support.


Assuming all (or most) UNOs here have other primary jobs, what ways are you exploring to effectively manage communication with your license operators?


As the primary owner/operator of another business, I’m considering hiring a part-time community manager to run a Discord server. I’m also considering setting aside a block of hours each week where I’m personally available to answer questions.


If questions pop up that I don’t have a timely answer to, I’ll reach out directly to Unity support and also run a search here in the official UNO TG to see if the problem already has a solution.


**What will you be doing?**

author: Quoia75

Aware - and that's necessary I think, but if you are managing hundreds (or thousands) of licenses, that can quickly get crazy.

When the API becomes available I'm going to explore having questions routed to a Discord server for easier management. 👌

author: Quoia75
msg:
**Another question for any of you who have hired community managers** (I have not but as stated, exploring):


Just from discussions with other Web 3 contractors, I notice that people hired for that role prefer less traditional communication. So for example, I’m use to having bi-monthly video calls with my sub-contractors for content and web development stuff. 


People hired for community management roles tend to prefer pure messaging or email comms (?)


Does that track with anyone else?


Just making sure this older Web 2 dev is keeping up with what’s best for the Web 3 workforce.

author: Serhii Voloshyn
msg:
Here's why this problem arose and why static IP solved it:
1. The problem of "collective responsibility" (CGNAT)
Your previous address (100.64.107.114) was an internal address of the provider. This means that hundreds or even thousands of subscribers were accessing the Internet through the same common public IP address.
What happened: If someone else from your city (who is on the same provider) was doing suspicious things, sending spam, or simply updating the application too often, the server's security systems (Firewall/WAF) put this common IP address on the "blacklist".
Result: When you tried to connect, the server saw a "banned" address and gave you a 403 Forbidden, although you personally did not violate anything.
2. Specifics of the WebSocket protocol
Applications often use WebSocket for instant messages or updates. Unlike regular websites, WebSocket requires a long and stable connection.
Provider gateways (NAT) that serve dynamic addresses often "aggressively" terminate old connections to make room for new users.
The application server could view your router's attempt to break through this "double barrier" as an attack or a malformed request.

3. Why did it work before and then stop?
This could happen for three reasons:
Address pool change: The provider could have changed the routing, and you ended up in a subnet that was already in the spam filter database.
Application security update: Developers could have tightened security rules by prohibiting connections from addresses belonging to large provider networks (this is often done to combat bots).
Provider neighbor: Someone from your network segment began to show activity that activated server protection.

author: Joshua F
msg:
Thanks Calus, at the moment it’s hard for me to tell whether Unity is a fully dedicated standalone effort, or something being run alongside other MN/WM priorities with staff split across multiple workstreams.

As a UNO, that leaves me unclear on where Unity is heading and when, who owns what, and how we should plan or support over the next 6–12 months. It also feels like a number of essential roles and product features are still missing, without a clear plan or timeline for when they’ll land.

I’m optimistic about what Unity can achieve, but feel like some transparency here from the team would really help to tie everything together and allow us to properly prepare for whats ahead.

author: Calus B
msg:
So it’s a joint venture between world, Mobile, and and network as you probably know. It is split I believe 50-50 between the two companies. It exists to serve customers that minutes network and world mobile individually cannot serve. As such you will see both World mobile and minutes  network teams. Interacting. 
Specifically Unity will have its own sales and onboarding teams for its clients. Josh overseas much of the technical details as well as other members of the minutes teams (Omri  Dork ect) Jamie is working hard on many of the partnerships in web 3. They do push a lot of the grunt work out to third-party vendors as well. Josh has mentioned this previously. 

Unity is at its core supposed to be a for-profit business that pays the node operators to manage licenses and then provisions tasks for those license operators to do. They are sort of the facilitators making high-quality testing environments for telecom companies. Obviously on the network side, they’re using the sharing economy, but on the telecom side, they are working more as a traditional business. It’s sort of a hybrid. 

To somewhat address, your concerns about where we are going and how fast we are going there at least from an ambassador level I will say this. Unity is only possible because of the maturation of world mobile and minutes network. It is an absolutely necessary part of their business model. I would expect the highest levels of attention to be paid on it. I know at this point they haven’t shared many details about their plans. We’ve seen scout and runner we’ve seen some CLI tasks and some basic telemetry tasks. They’ve hinted at many more tasks that will be rolled out. I think these are the tasks that you will see over the next 6 to 12 months. Beyond that as the network matures, they have some broader plans to help support license operators specifically those who are doing manual tasks as well with additional ability to earn ups. The beauty of what they’re building is it can be utilized in so many broad applications once it’s going. It’s going to be one of the Crown Jewels of this ecosystem they are building.

author: Joshua F
msg:
If UNOs are effectively Unity’s franchisees, then they’re not setting us up to succeed yet. We’re expected to market and do licensee management but there’s still very limited guidance, tools and training, unclear comms, and little forward visibility so we’re left speculating.

It seems like this speculation  is increasingly fuelling negative sentiment, which can spill online and kneecap the project early. Don’t want to be pessimistic but this verified group feels more like sentiment contagion than benefit to us UNOs. I’ve got strong confidence in the team delivering the technical telecoms side, I just think the franchise/community layer needs the same level of attention, with clearer expectations and enablement so we can do the job we’ve paid to do.

author: Quoia75
msg:
**🌈Dream scenario – A Node Management Website that would allow UNOs to:**

- Easily move batches of licenses between nodes. In that way nodes could be treated like folders for managing node-specific splits for reward tiers or groups of ULOs.

- Assign a custom name to each node.

- Assign a unique QR Code to each node that when scanned by a new ULO would automatically generate a lease code that’s forwarded to the ULO via email or SMS.

- Create and assign a ticker to the front of licenses so they are easy to find by a UNOs own ULOs browsing the marketplace.

- Track earnings by license / by node on an Analytics UI

- A License/node health monitor UI

- Allow reports to be generated and downloaded as CSV files or PDFs

- Allow the bulk generation of lease codes


….what am I missing?

I would rather see referrer groups.

Each referrer group can have its own unique split 

Single link for a referrer group to pick up a license from your pool of available licenses

I think because the approach to marketing and license deployment is going to be so varied given each node owner's goals, the release of a public API will be the ultimate solution, while some basic prized features like bulk license management should be baked in.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: I'm doing one for ULOs in a  Facebook group. Similar idea to support my ULOs/promote licenses. I'm thinking that any UNO will be welcome to contribute  by answering questions etc and promote their own licenses in exchange. Thinking through the details now. Something like, you are welcome to add a link to your own licenses at the bottom of every useful comment. ' if you found this helpful, please consider leasing one of my licences @', sort of thing. I will promote my own licences at a 50/50 split. This would need to be the minimum to prevent the usual race to the. I'm happy to pick up licences both actively and passively from this if the group is active. Happy for other UNOs to benefit from the platform, particularly if we can pick up a critical mass. What do you think? I've also got a Facebook page which I will announce later today or tomorrow.

msg:
**❓Question UNOs:**


I purchased an extra node with the intention of transferring it to another person. That person is now saying they would like me to retain ownership of the node, but they want to have the ability to manage and distribute the node's licenses to ULOs.


Is this possible? (probably a silly question). If it is, can any of you describe how this would be achieved and tracked? Do any of you have a similar arrangement?


TY

author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - CLI Mini Guide** 

A  quick guide to the new CLI opt-in task.

𝗢𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗲𝘄:
CLI tasks require an active Unity license
CLI is an opt-in task, and license operators must enable it
CLI calls are live
Calls are real and non-synthetic
CLI calls look like a regular incoming call
CLI works in every region
Tasks are carrier-agnostic
Any SIM or eSIM that can receive incoming calls is supported
Compatible with iOS, even if the “Unity connection” task shows offline 

𝗡𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀:
When a CLI call is received, users get a notification
Notifications include a push notification and an in-app pop-up

𝗠𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗱 𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗹𝘀**:**
It’s OK if you miss the call
You can report a CLI call up to 24 hours after it’s received

𝗦𝗜𝗠** **𝗯𝗮𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 / 𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲:
If your SIM or eSIM can receive incoming calls, it can accept CLI tests
You usually do NOT need an active balance on your SIM/eSIM to receive a CLI call, but this depends on your carrier, and some carriers may require it

𝗡𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸 𝗜𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲𝘀:
Unity Points are distributed immediately after a valid CLI task is completed 

CLI – verifying telecom activity at scale.

🔗 https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2020940460231995656

🤝 **Official Unity** **Resources**
[**Unity**](https://unitynodes.io/) | [**Unity Litepaper**](https://unitynodes.io/documents/litepaper.pdf) | [**QR Code Login**](https://uno.unitynodes.io/) | [**Manage**](https://manage.unitynodes.io/) | [**Telegram**](https://t.me/unitynodes) | [**X**](https://x.com/UnityNodesIO) | [**YouTube**](https://youtube.com/@UnityNodesIO) | [**Bug Report**](https://unitynodes.io/bug-report) | [**Verification Bot**](https://t.me/UnityNetworkVerificationBot)

author: Sören Lewis Müller
msg:
On my end in average rewards look good again across all ULOs (mostly 70/30)

Including $0.00 payouts:

Average (mean): $0.1404

Median: $0.17

25th percentile (Q1): $0.1225

75th percentile (Q3): $0.20

Std dev: $0.0761 (inflated by the many $0.00s)

Excluding $0.00 payouts (only wallets that actually earned something):

# non-zero payouts: 173 (so 37 were $0.00)

Mean (non-zero): $0.1704

Median (non-zero): $0.17

Q1/Q3 (non-zero): $0.15 / $0.20

Std dev (non-zero): $0.0437 (much tighter)

Interpretation: the “real” earning cluster is very tight around $0.15–$0.20, and the main distortion is simply the $0.00 group.

I switched to Vodaphone and rewards have jumped to 0.25 to 0.33!! Turns out the Virgin media router was using Dynamic IP address which change! The Vodaphone router has static IP address which really helped! If i was you i would look into how ur router is set up

author: Val | WhiteBridge Noderunners | MNT SN.VN.UN
msg:
Static or dynamic IP should be irrelevant 

This project is set up to dynamic ranges as ULOs worldwide will often change IP once they go offline

The biggest question is the vpn/proxy firewall 
If they use a DNS blocking that could affect complete IP ranges many will have connection issues

IP limitations will also be an issue if limits are per IP instead of per license, this seems to be the case but we’ll need more data to analyze it

More tasks more requests means more work to be done helping those with multiple phones on 1 location
For telemetry it’s just terrible as the data will all be the same and therefore less profitable

author: Opher
msg:
I have (4) 5g router ISP’s with 2 boxes on each that were starting to only receive .02 - .01 . But I have 20 complete phones connected on WiFi on a 5g router at a different location getting an average of .20 each, definitely down from the .30 average. So I’m going to split farm boxes on their own isp for now since it seems 20 devices and under is somewhat productive. I have 4 ISP’s currently, but I’ll double it for the time being and have 4 boxes/4 ISP’s in location 1 and 4 boxes/ 4 ISPs in location 2. 

My cost for the (8) different ISPs will only be $260 a month.
The future tense is to hope things get resolved and more than 1 box can be run on a single isp and I can just double up again on each of the 8 ISPs. 

I’m sure some might find it pointless to try harder. But I don’t have much to lose anyway. Doesn’t hurt to try I guess

author: capri
msg:
For the last 3 days rewards dropped ~10x for almost everyone.
No explanation, no response.

It’s clearly not our settings, routers, modems, or ISP.

As a form of protest, I propose that we shut down immediately our farms until the rewards return to normal levels or we receive a clear and appropriate explanation.

The team may not officially support our farms, but the network still depends on our infrastructure. If we cannot even cover our operational costs, there is no reason for us to continue running. At this point, we have nothing to lose.

At least I will shut down mine but together we are stronger

author: David P [DPADA]
msg:
I’m going to post this probably last time. Because I have already shared this multiple times with people but no body really wants to believe it or listen.

From all my testing. I believe there is some sort of filtering happening.

Where they will take a snapshot before rewards are paid.

If your setups match the following criteria’s. You will get good rewards:

- 1 connection / 1 carrier ISP per 20 units. Doesn’t matter what type carrier service, fibre, sim router, star link whatever. MAX 20 devices.

- And your uptime per license.

You will get rewards between .15 to .25 UPS per unit.


As for the word limiting and restricting number of units.

Technically they have restricted them, you can run as many devices as you want but guess what your rewards will just be .0008.

So you basically don’t get paid but by the giving you something they technically are rewarding still farms.


Now I don’t know if this is temporary or the permanent situation, but it is the situation right now.

And because of severe lack of communication and even when it has been desperately asked for we have nothing formally from Unity team.

Do what you want with this info. Everyone will do what makes sense for them.

Love you guys! Stay healthy,
Stay safe!

author: David P [DPADA]
msg:
One last thing:

It doesn’t have to be 24hrs of above.

I changed my setups like 6hrs before payouts and it instantly changed the results.

Which is why the snapshot and filtering is doing rewards. 

Change as soon as you can and do your own tests to confirm.

author: Opher
msg:
Just looked at rewards and I see some interesting reactions to my reconfiguration from 1-2 days ago. 
I had 6 Boxes on 2 ISP’s. Originally getting the full rewards, until recently. So now I have an ISP for every box. 4 boxes are in location 1 and 2 boxes in location 2. The 2 boxes in location 2 received .16 - .24 with the 2 NEW ISP’s. The 4 boxes in location 1 received mixed results. For example, 2 boxes with 2 new ISP’s got .16 - .24 . But 2 boxes that were on the older existing ISP’s only got .01 - .03 . Kind of weird how the already existing ISP’s that previously had 3 boxes each attached was still receiving .01 - .03 with only one box attached to it now, when my other 4 exact setups with NEW ISP’s are getting .16 - .24. I went ahead and factory reset those 2 older( older as in already existing before the new ones) gateways, reset the 2 boxes and set them back up to the gateways. So maybe that’ll refresh those two systems..

author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg: Nice instruction to create reward dashboard below:

author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg: [Photo] Yes prety much go to you UNO account —-> connect your wallet —> right click ——> select inspect ——> a whole dashboard with data will load up —-> select the tab "network" —-> make sure :fetch/XHR is selected —-> click on "reload page" (if not info is loaded) —-> right click on "rewards_get_allocations" not "...balance" not "...summary" —-> "copy response" Thats where the GOLD data is. Copy and paste it in a .txt file save it and use that data to prompt Claude: Could you please create a rewards dashboard from my Unity account (https://manage.unitynodes.io/rewards/allocation) using the data attached (.txt file) and from it, create a chrome plug-in to refresh data when needed.

author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg:
what's claude?
https://claude.ai/login

author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg: [Photo] Claude will create a zip chrome plug-in file, download it, unzip, go to chrome extensions, enable on the top right side "developer mode" and click on "load unpacked" select the recent chrome extension file generated by cloude, pin it to your chrome so you have it handy AND BOILA!

author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg: If there is an error with the plugin take a screenshot and show the info to claude he will fix it and redo the file, so unistall the bad one and load the new one again. I also prompt it to redo the plugin  pairing the last 4 of the licenses with the names I have on my excel sheet( I just copy and pasted that data and now I can see the labels/names I used to identify it faster

author: Daniel 🇷🇴
msg:
it's a thing baked in Android: Internet connection priority
default priority (most devices):
- Wi-Fi
- Mobile data (cellular)
- USB tethering (Ethernet via USB)

You can change the order in rooted devices

author: Danny | World Market | ENO UK
msg:
After testing different device counts per box, here are the averages I’ve recorded (per device):
1 device → ~0.33 U
2 devices → ~0.26 U
3 devices → ~0.22 U
4 devices → ~0.21 U
5 devices → ~0.219 U (previous test average)
10 devices → 0.20 U average
14 devices → 0.2009 U average (2sims)


🔎 Key takeaway:
There is clear dilution as more devices share the same connection, but it’s not perfectly linear. Some mid-range setups (4–14 devices) appear to balance yield vs cost more efficiently.

author: Opher
msg: I disabled IPv6, Gave all 20 motherboards static IPs, lowered MTU size on Gateway, enabled UPnP, minimized firewall rules to the lowest security possible, and rebooted both gateway + box. author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg:
🎉🧧 Happy Lunar New Year, Unity Farmers! 🧧🎉

Wishing you and your families a healthy, prosperous, and powerful year ahead. May your farms stay stable, your rewards steady, and your setups optimized 🚀

As we start the new year, just a friendly reminder:
Please be mindful about sharing detailed farming strategies, reward data from farms, configurations, or internal insights in the official Unity channels.

We’ll be a bit stricter on this moving forward — not to limit discussion, but to protect the community and avoid the team introducing additional restrictive measures based on publicly shared information.

Let’s keep growing smart, stay low-key where needed, and support each other responsibly.

Have a great New Year everyone! 🐎✨

Is the reward system locked in my country or something? I have 190 active licenses, but in 7 days I have only earned $27 — that’s not even $4 per day.

I have a friend in the United States, and each license there still earns around $0.15–$0.20 per day on average. However, in my country, it is only $0.0x per day.

author: $UPER-NODES.io
msg: What do you mean exactly? Hopefully my questions will be answered during next weeks UNO-AMA.

author: $UPER-NODES.io
msg:
#amaquestions
1) Are the rewards that are currently being earned per ULO mainly rewards for being online and for a certain uptime or is the Unity App performing real customer assignments? If assignments what assignments are being done? 2) What are the tiers (#) of ULO’s perIP-address (or SIM) before the rewards are reduced? 3) I would like to propose the possibility to run more ULO’s (say max 120 ULO’s) per IP-address before the rewards per ULO will be punished. 120 ULO < 0,01% of the available ULO. This to keep word with the earlier stated FAQ-question (see below) and to make it possible to run a small company. For me max 0,01% per IP is still superdecentralized and distributed! What is your idea about this proposal? Q: Are there any limits on how many Unity Operator Software licenses canbe operated from a single location? A: There is no limit to the number of Unity License Operators that canrun from a single location. However, we do not encourage this.
